title: Avoid declaring variables and immediately returning or throwing them
description: Declaring a variable only to immediately return or throw it is considered a bad practice because it adds unnecessary complexity to the code. This practice can make the code harder to read and understand, as it introduces an extra step that doesn't add any value. Instead of declaring a variable and then immediately returning or throwing it, it is generally better to return or throw the value directly. This makes the code cleaner, simpler, and easier to understand.
recommendation: Return or throw the value directly
impact: Makes the code harder to read and understand

checks.message: Return or throw the value directly
